pattern.name: Unsafe direct dict/attribute access on nullable

incidents.title: Payment API 500 errors on empty cart checkout
incidents.root_cause_summary: Payment handler accessed cart.items[0] without checking for empty cart, causing IndexError when users clicked checkout with an empty cart.
incidents.fix_strategy: Guard cart.items access with empty check, return 400 for empty carts

patterns.name: Unsafe direct dict/attribute access on nullable
patterns.description: Accessing properties or indices on a value that can be None/null/empty without a guard check

lessons_learned: Always guard collection access with empty/null checks
API endpoints should validate input state before processing
Empty state is a common edge case that needs explicit test coverage

incidents.title: Database connection pool exhaustion under load
incidents.root_cause_summary: Connection pool max_size was set to 10 in config but the new async worker count was raised to 50, causing pool starvation under normal load.
incidents.fix_strategy: Align pool max_size with worker count, add pool exhaustion monitoring alert

patterns.name: Resource limit mismatch between components
patterns.description: Configuration values for resource limits are inconsistent across dependent components

patterns.name: Config drift between deployment parameters
patterns.description: Deployment configuration changed without updating dependent resource limits

lessons_learned: Resource limits should be validated against dependent component configurations
Add CI checks that verify config consistency across components
Pool exhaustion should trigger alerts before reaching 100%

incidents.title: User listing crash on empty database
incidents.root_cause_summary: PR #247 optimization replaced safe .get() with direct dict access on query result, causing TypeError when database returns empty result set.
incidents.fix_strategy: Restore null-safe access pattern: use conditional check before accessing first_user properties

patterns.name: Unsafe direct dict/attribute access on nullable
patterns.description: Accessing properties or indices on a value that can be None/null/empty without a guard check
patterns.signature: result[key] without None check after optimization removed .get()

patterns.name: Premature optimization removing safety guards
patterns.description: Performance optimization that removes null/error safety checks
patterns.signature: .get() replaced with direct [] access for performance

lessons_learned: Optimizations that remove safety checks should require explicit empty-state tests
Code review should flag removal of .get() / optional chaining as a risk
This pattern has recurred — consider adding a linter rule
